level_signals: Minimum 6–8 years of combined program management and analytical experience
Reports to Sr. Manager, Quality Assurance

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 6–8 years of combined program management and analytical experience, preferably in customer experience, contact center operations, trust and safety, or comparable operational functions.
Experience in Customer Experience, contact center, quality assurance, or trust and safety operations.
Understanding of contact center metrics (quality scores, sentiment, first contact resolution, average handle time) and the operational levers that move them.
Understanding of A/B testing and other forms of statistical analysis.
Proficiency with AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot) and a demonstrated ability to integrate them into day-to-day workflows.
Demonstrated experience as both a program manager and a hands-on data analyst — not one supported by the other.
High proficiency in SQL, with experience writing complex queries, joins, and optimizations against large datasets.
Experience with analytical visualization tools such as Mode, Tableau, Looker, Sigma, or similar tools.
Track record of building reporting and analytics that an executive audience actually uses to make decisions.
Proven ability to run cross-functional programs with named owners, published service-level agreements, and measurable outcomes.
Ability to identify potential root causes contributing to changes in quality and efficiency metrics and provide recommendations on mitigation strategy.
Extremely strong verbal and written communication skills, including the ability to synthesize complex topics and create compelling narratives for various audiences.

Ability to work effectively with internal stakeholders, including data scientists, data engineers, and operational leaders. Work cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Operations, and L&D to drive change.
High level of accountability and ownership — driven and focused self-starter.
Strategic mindset — the ability to think ahead of where the program is at now and help stand up a new operating model rather than maintain an established one.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with QA operations: rubric design and calibration, auditor variance management, dispute workflows, and core quality scoring methodologies.
Working knowledge of QA platforms such as Kaizo, MaestroQA, Playvox, or comparable tools.
Exposure to LLM-assisted auditing, automated quality scoring, or other applied AI tooling within a customer experience context.
Experience with R or Python (fluency in at least one preferred).
Experience with experimentation, data modeling, ETL, and data pipeline development.

scope.surface: Quality Assurance program within Instacart's global Customer Experience organization — covering customer, retailer, and shopper interaction channels
scope.business_model: marketplace / gig-economy grocery delivery (B2C and B2B)
core_responsibilities: Operate QA cadences end-to-end (daily anomaly standups, weekly quality reviews, monthly business reviews, quarterly rubric calibration sessions)
Manage central actions tracker routing quality signals to named owners across five action workstreams
Publish and enforce service-level agreements; surface adherence to leadership weekly
Build and maintain QA dashboards and reporting surfacing critical KPIs
Write SQL queries against contact and audit data to investigate spikes and answer ad-hoc business questions
Analyze audit data, customer sentiment, and contact-driver patterns to identify systemic issues
Partner with Data Science and Analytics to design SSOT dashboards and reporting models
Develop forward-looking glide-path and impact models for quarterly goal-setting
Build automated reporting systems to keep CX and operations leaders informed
Collaborate with Engineering, Product, L&D, Automation, and Operations on quality signal routing and outcomes measurement
Communicate outcomes and insights to cross-functional stakeholders including senior leadership

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant and annual refresh grants

green_flags: Equity mentioned (new hire grant + annual refresh)
Flex First remote policy — fully remote eligible
Role has clear, concrete ownership with defined SLA targets and KPIs
Detailed JD with specific metrics targets (≥95% adherence, ≤7 days escalation cycle, ≤5 days signal-to-action lag) signals an operationally mature team
Role is building a new operating model, not just maintaining an existing one — suggests high-impact opportunity
red_flags: Hiring restricted to four Canadian provinces only (Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Nova Scotia) — eliminates candidates outside those regions
Compensation stated in CAD only; no USD range provided, suggesting this is a Canada-only remote role despite '#LI-Remote' tag
6–8 years minimum is a wide band that could indicate role level uncertainty
Dual requirement (program manager AND hands-on data analyst) is demanding and narrows the candidate pool significantly

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from 'currently only hiring in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Nova Scotia')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and SLA targets in JD body)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; CAD range captured as base_range_cad instead)
seniority.level (inferred from title, years required, and reporting structure)
ambiguities: The JD is tagged '#LI-Remote' suggesting remote-global, but the body explicitly restricts hiring to four Canadian provinces (Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Nova Scotia). Treated as remote-global with a Canada-only geographic restriction; location.remote_us_eligible set to false.

The years range '6–8 years' is stated as a minimum qualification, which is unusual (ranges typically denote preferred band not a floor). Captured minimum_value as 6 and maximum as 8 per the stated text.
work_auth and visa_sponsorship are not explicitly addressed in the JD body; left as 'unclear'.
